text: I would like to invite on stage somebody we would like to request her to honor,
Ms. Irani. She's such an achiever in her own right. Where do we lose? To my left, Sudha Murthy.
The amazing legend herself, Sudha Murthy. A philanthropist, a storyteller, and now a
parliamentarian. And she's going to be our next speaker, but I thought in this sort of,
you know, where else would you get a stage where you had Karan Johar Smriti Irani
and Sudha Murthy together? I think we just need to get Karan into politics as well and then
everything would be perfect. And ma'am, may I request you to do the honors and present Ms. Irani
with our Changemaker Award. Can we have a big, big, big round of applause? Louder, louder.
Would you like to say something? I know your session is next, but about women in the public space.
About Smriti Ji. Of course, sir. She's a woman with courage. She's a great fighter and fantastic
actress. I want to tell one thing about Karan. Though you were asking rapid fire, you know,
I was thinking, which is the most memorable song in Bollywood? Suraj hua madhyam chand dal nahi.
It's a fantastic ball. Very beautiful ball. Thank you ma'am. That means the world to me. Thank you so
much. I'm sure Shah Rukh Khan will also be very happy. Of course, Kajol will be. Yes, of course.
Well, we are going to take a 10 to 15 minute break and Sudha Murthy, the amazing Sudha
Murthy, the next speaker, before we let them off stage, a wonderful, loud round of applause
for all our wonderful speakers.
Hi everyone. Thank you very much for your patience. I apologize to anybody who couldn't get in, who had
a bit of a struggle to get in. We have had more than a house full. So it's a problem of plenty.
I apologize to anybody who had a bit of difficulty getting in. We were trying to give everybody a
spot. Thank you so much for showing us so much love and so much support and so much interest in
We the Women. Welcome back everybody. How are we doing this evening?
So let's start on a happy note because we've had a happy day.
So let's have like a round of applause just for ourselves.
My next guest is a woman of so many parts that I sometimes wonder is there anything that Sudha
Murthy cannot do. She is today a member of India's parliament, but she's a prolific
best-selling author. I'm not sure how many of you know that her 10,000 rupees was the first
investment in Infosys. She was one of the, one of the name, the first female engineer to be hired
at Tata. She's a philanthropist. She's an incredible human being. She wears her wealth
and her achievement very lightly, even when she has been the mother-in-law to the prime minister
of the United Kingdom. So she has a special London connection. Please put your hands together to
welcome Sudha Murthy on stage. We can give her a rousing welcome.
Let's give her a big applause because she's been, she's been waiting a long time to meet all of you
and I have tested her patience so apologies for that ma'am. So many people here to meet you so we
had to take a little bit of time to get everybody in. Can I request you to take the mic ma'am? Yes.
So Sudha ma'am, what is it that Sudha Murthy can't do? I have lost count of the number of things you
have done. Engineer, philanthropist, storyteller, parliamentarian, I mean I'm losing count.
What else can't you do?
Many things I can't do. Like? I can't cook very well.
That is the reason my husband has maintained his weight.
Does he cook? He's better than me. Okay. What else can't you do?
Well I can't swim because I grew up in an area where there was a water scarcity. So I never
learned swimming and if much when my children were young they wanted anything from me they said,
amma you get in, when I'm you know they said okay I'll get you into the swimming pool that's the
best way to scare me. I'm not good in that. I'm not, I'm scared of swimming. Even now?
You know because I'm not used to that much water. Tell everybody about why you sent GRD Tata
a postcard accusing him of discrimination and how you ended up being the first female engineer.
It's a story about you that some of us know but not everybody knows.
First female engineer at Tata's and this is after writing a letter to GRD Tata.
I think it's on the website of Tata's everybody knows.
But you tell us no.
Okay from the horses. Yes.
Yes. I was young and I was academically very good. I have written an article actually,
how to beat boys because I joined engineering college I was the only girl in the university
and boys wouldn't talk to me. It was way back in 1968 you know. And I come from a small family
known as a Brahmin Madhva family. A very small community where women can become either teachers
or college professors. Sometimes doctors not more than that. So it was a great difficulty I did my
engineering and it was in the year 1974 in the month of April I suppose. I saw an advertisement
in Indian Institute of Science which is popularly known as the Tata Institute.
We have some of your old pictures up by the way on the screen.
Yeah that is when I was here. Once upon a time.
I saw an advertisement on the notice board saying that Tata's require telco.
Telco Tata company requires young bright engineers with a very good salary stipend etc.
Hard working etc. they gave and at the end they wrote ladies students need not apply.
They're like you know when you have a cigarette pack it's written there
this is a great cigarette but at the end injurious to health in a small way. I was very upset because
I was young. You know I get upset if somebody says the girls cannot do. Yes. I said why women
cannot do? What is that women cannot do? What women can do men cannot do according to me.
Like women can deliver children. Women can deliver children men cannot. I said okay let me write a
letter to them saying that they are wrong. I used to see J.R.D. Tata once in a year who is to come
to Indian Institute of Science for the Jamshedji's birthday 15th of March. And from a distance I used
to see because I'm a small town girl I'm not from a big town not from the Bombay, Delhi. I came from
a town known as Hubli with hardly two lakhs population. In that time it was one lakh population.
I used to see from a distance and I always felt how handsome he is. He had blue eyes and he had a
French mother actually. Very handsome and no courage to go and talk to him because he was the
company's head. So I thought let me write a letter to J.R.D. Tata because it is something wrong.
And why there's a gender discrimination? So I took a postcard in those days you
stay at a postcard for five paisa. Five paisa not even there. I wrote Mr. J.R.D. Tata I was surprised
that there's a discrimination of gender in your company. You know Tata company is ahead of time
and you your company established iron and steel locomotive Tata salt and many other things.
If somebody else would have done this one that I understand but not you. And please remember in a
society where women are 50 percent and you say women need not apply that means you are not
harnessing you are not unleashed the potential of 50 percent of the population and how such country
can progress.
And I signed my maiden name was Kulkarni Sudha Kulkarni. I signed and I did not know his address.
You know I was so innocent and simple. I said what is his address? I don't know. Mr. J.R.D. Tata
and which company wrote this one? Telco company. Telco Bombay that's all I posted. You know the
entirely wrong address. The real address was Mr. you know not even J.R.D. Tata the chairman of the
company was Mr. S. Mulgaonkar. S. Mulgaonkar chairman Telco 24 Homi Modi street Tamarind lane
Fort Bombay one. This is the correct address. I just posted but it is Tata you know the postman
went and gave that letter to J.R.D. Tata. He read that and he was very upset. He called all
these people you know who were in H.R.D. Did you advertise like that? They said yes we did that.
Why did you do that? They said sir we have to have a training of that person in Jamshedpur Bihar
and there is no lady other than Indira Gandhi who inaugurated the Jamshedpur plant. For one
hour she came and inaugurated. How can we have a girl who doesn't I mean where she can stay?
How she can work in shift? So we have that. He said no you call her for an interview. You test
her technically and if she is not good then we will talk about it. Actually I wrote one step to
him one letter one line to him. There's always a 10,000 lead journey but there's always one step
to start. It is Hoenad Sangh who wrote in 629 A.D. that there's always one step to start new things.
So he said the girl has written this. Whosoever that girl called for an interview and then we'll
see. Then they called me for an interview. In those days in ladies hostel there was no phone
because they used to think girls will talk to boys okay otherwise also they will talk they never
knew. So there was a we used to get a telegram that Kulkarni should you know come and attend
the interview and thus I attended the interview. All my hostel mates said we'll give you 30 rupees
each. You get a saree in Pune they are very good so go and get some 7-8 saris. I go there and then
I saw the interview panel. I was the only girl who was interviewed at a very good technical interview
and I passed out passed and then he said you know Kulkarni why we don't want to take girl
these are the following reasons. I said one or other day you should start. You cannot give reasons
you know you make a girl's hostel or you make a special arrangement for a girl until many
girls will come but this is not the reason. Then they said fine we'll let you know but
what happens to a girl when she gets a job and we give her training for two years
and she gets married our training will be a waste. I said wait a minute you train a boy for two years
and he gets another 500 rupees more somewhere else he will leave and go. So you cannot say that one
and they said you're selected. I came back I got admission in America for to do my PhD
and I told my father when I got down and told him from a train you know he came to meet me he said
you know dad I wrote a J.R.D. Tata letter they called me for an interview all these stories. I
thought my father will appreciate he said you don't have brain he told me first thing. How can you
write a person like J.R.D. Tata postcard you have to do in a bundle of file. Second thing he told me
are you going to take up this job. I said no I'm not taking this job I'm going to US.
He said I'm really disappointed they had their policies they made their offer you you talk to
them you should take you know it's not fair they have given you a chance to make it fair and you
will run away. He said I'm so sure I will give in writing you go to America you'll do your PhD
you'll meet somebody there you'll marry and you won't come back and then why did you talk this.
I always brought up my children what they talk they have to walk you have not done that
and however decision is yours. It took two days for me that I should go to America or take up
this job yes or no. Today they say I want my space and I said I want my time to think.
I took today's time then I with tears in my eyes I tore the admission letter of US and I joined
Tata because I asked them why you should not and when they gave me a chance I should show I should
work like any other person. I never showed my card I'm a female I cannot do no I always like
work like any other person except when I was carrying Akshata that time I told sir
you know I want the meeting in the ground floor you know other than that I never showed my thing
that oh I'm a female I can't do no. Did you it's an extraordinary story but did you feel bad that
you could not go to America? Well when I was young once upon a time you know I was young I felt I
should have done once I went to US first time in 1979 I saw a mighty and I said what a great
place I should have done this one for a few hours I felt bad but you know later I realized it is not
the institute or anything it is you you can make the change you are the person you are responsible
for your life. That what Krishna said in Bhagavad Gita who is your best friend you yourself
who is your worst enemy you yourself so ultimately if I want to do anything in life
I am responsible and I'll make it. Your 10 000 rupees that you earned as a Tata engineer
making history as the first woman engineer to be employed is what you give to Narayan
Murthy and his friends as they want to start a company called Infosys. Did they ever thank you
enough for those 10 000 rupees? Well actually you asked me what I'm not good at it I'm not good
I'm not good at as an economist I'm not I'm not good in you know investing money and all I'm really
not good at I know how to spend but not how to invest that's the last thing I do in my life
because I don't have inclination for that so Mr. Murthy told me you know he wants to he said one
day he told me he's very smart he told me my grandfather was a history teacher so he always
grew up with history he said you know when there was industrialization in India that time we did
not get the benefit of it today the software is going to be the next you know next thing which
is going to change India's future look at the past if you want to be legally ethically rich this
is the one way you can do this start a software company I said that looks the words look great
you can start but he said I want money I said I don't have that kind of money he said whatever
you have at least give me that money and I will start my company I thought or I didn't say
immediately yes I thought or it women think more than men you know all actually men think they are
smarter than men in this room they should excuse me women are smarter than men I always notice
because nature makes her smart she's a mother she's a mother-in-law she's a daughter-in-law
she's a daughter she's a sister she has so many human relations and she has to manage all and
that makes her very smart my when I got married my mother told me a word you know without your
husband's knowledge in a middle-class family that is taught you know in your in your life without
your husband's knowledge save some money keep it separate and never husband should never know about
it and this money should not be used for ornaments or silk saris or anything it is for emergency
okay nine one one that kind okay nine one one when you're you know and otherwise you should not
talk about it at all I thought about it I said what murthy is asking some money and what he's
asking not for any other thing it is not that I'm wasting money buying something he wants to start
a company and it looks nice but I'm not sure that company will take off or not but it is better to
be in life better to you know think regret is worse than trying new things I said in case I
don't give then he may not start if he doesn't start throughout his life he will have regret I
wish I could have done I said let him not regret it is a kind of adventure he's starting and it
was Thursday morning at seven o'clock I said I have actually I had 10 250 rupees you still remember
this so many years later I gave only 10 000 rupees what did you do with the other 200 rupees 250 I kept
it by the time my daughter Akshata was born I said in case there's any medical emergency anything
later let me have 250 rupees that's the way how a finance person should look after the family
then I told murthy look you have good experience it is like a circus you know from one one wing of
the circus or one end of the circus you jump to the other end but there's always a safety net
because that is in your mind I'll be your safety net and take this 10 000 rupees I gave but I'm
the best investor probably in the world because I got it maybe how many times I don't know you
people know in the press I got it that much money back amazing story now Mr. Murthy started a big
national debate with his belief that when you want to build something you have to work very hard
sometimes you have to work 70 hours a week or more how many hours a week do you work
well maybe 90 hours or 100 hours a week I don't even know and now I definitely work more than 90
hours a week more than 90 hours at this age also because I have a lot of commitment and one of them
is like you know in in parliament I study the questions I make research and then I will ask
questions then I also write you know and for writing also I do research plus I have my own
constituency I have to go and sit with them and find out and how to travel to villages I work very
hard and and what would you say to younger people today who say all you they say to me also you
people are just obsessed with your work you don't have any other life what would you say to them
when they say that actually you know there is no shortcut to success please remember you have to
work very hard you should have resilience and still you can manage looking after the family
I have done that one like Murthy used to work 90 hours or 100 hours you know he used to travel
200 days 220 days in a year for many years we never had a holiday in 20 years of our marriage
at that time after that once we went to Greece or something because I love history after that we
hardly go on in 47 48 years you've been on one holiday only one honest that is also on a study
holiday like I wanted to see Greece Murthy wanted to sleep okay but by and large no but I don't have
regret see we come from a middle class family we don't have any connection nor money nothing of that
when you want to build a world-class company like Infosys we don't have a magic wand of
Cinderella so how you can do you can do only with hard work and that a wife should understand
my husband is working 18 hours 16 hours a day and I should look after the family I was the mother
and father for my both children Murthy came only for the convocation but I used to go for a PTA
meeting talk with the teachers how they do well they don't do well yes it is your duty because
your husband is doing a great tapas for a betterment of your family but what if it's
what if it's the other way around behind every successful woman there's an understanding man
because now I work like that I'm just not there either I'm in a constituency or you know four
months in Delhi or I'll be writing something and Murthy does the same thing he always supports me
when I became member of the parliament I asked Mr. Murthy you are no no he's four years older to me
you are 79 my sister is with me she's 77 I'm the youngest 75 okay baby in the family okay I said you
two are old do you agree if I go to Delhi and stay four months in a year and constituency I
have to look after I have to work hard if you agree Murthy said a very beautiful sentence
for sake of our country you know many people have become Shaheed Bhagat Singh gave his life
he was 23 or 22 yeah when we our country we got independence with a great price and God is kind
to us there are flights from Delhi to Bangalore yeah of course there's a you know roadblocks in
Bangalore you know all those things are there but there are flights are there you can come weekend
and remaining life of yours it should be dedicated to the country so we will stand behind you if you
have understanding in marriage you can do work-life balance if you have understanding in marriage it
is not number of hours you spend with your wife or husband you may spend and quality time you
should have you may get a lot of time with your husband and you may fight with him it is better
you have good understanding of your husband's work and he should understand his wife's priority
then it is possible both of you can achieve did you give your children Akshata and Rohan equal
freedom and equal opportunity or did you bring them up differently equal because my father brought
us equal poor my father he was a doctor and a professor when I joined engineering college
there was no girl in the whole university that that's the reason they said girls will
number one girls will not come even though the girl comes the courses are so tough and can you
they used to talk like this can you ever imagine a girl becoming engineer in 1968 so they never built
a toilet for a girl yeah because they said it's a waste of money yeah and my father sends me to
engineering college because I say I want to do engineering and in India what you get free is
advice everybody came and said doctor sir your middle doctor does not have bhaja or no this
awake portion is vacant she's a teenager she says she wants to engineering can you ever think a
girl doing engineering no like anytime do you know a man can give birth to a baby no similarly
a girl cannot become engineer that's the way how people used to think and don't believe her and
send her to engineering college she may run away with a boy they never knew there was narayan
murthy across the river i will marry him after you know seven eight years so they always felt a girl
will not complete she will fail and your three daughters the mental one is doing like this other
two girls will not get married because nobody will marry such thoughts were there and my father said
no my daughter is doing legally ethically right thing and i will support so my father always
believed both all children three daughters and the son all are equal same way i'd consider rohan and
akshita both are equal amazing and when your when when your daughter became the wife of a man who
became the prime minister of this country uh did you worry for akshita that you know suddenly life
will change much more public scrutiny you know how it happens everybody will have an opinion and
as a mother you must have felt protective and was that an odd feeling to be the sort of united
kingdom's mom and no see i always felt the positions come and positions go he's always my
dear son-in-law like a son to me i dedicated one book to receive to receive my new son okay so it
doesn't matter positions are not related to relationship second thing i told akshita life
will change and it is going to be harsh you should believe in yourself and let people talk anything
you should not bother it is hard because you are young but i have learned in my life let anybody
talk anything to me i will not bother as long as legally ethically it is right okay people will
always talk in foundation when i don't give money okay they said look at that lady many people have
talked narayan murthy's wife does is not bright you know but because her husband has money she
signs a check so she has become famous people talk like that i don't care if you give money they said
suza murthy you are so good that also i don't care both are wrong i do it because if the cause
is good i'll give money let people talk whatever they want and i will not live on people's opinions
i have never lived in people on people's opinion today you may say good tomorrow you may say bad
correct what i am inside that i'm the same person i know my defects i know my strength why should i
give my happiness in your hand and take it back and said oh barka told me i'm good so i'm good
no well if i'm good i'm good inside if i'm bad i'm bad inside and i want to reduce my bad qualities
yes that's amazing and i think it's it's the most brilliant advice to people just answer to
yourself don't take both praise and criticism lightly i have a last question to you
has money changed you in some ways people have changed with that with me for example everybody's
expectation has gone up for example uh in my life it has not affected me uh for me i eat the same
food uh and actually i become a lot more conscious not to spend money and other things
same money i can give it as a scholarship to a child that way i think but people's relation
has changed with respect to money with respect to me uh if i go to any wedding what gift i give it
is very important she's so rich she's giving us such a simple i give i give good books actually
he said what she should have given some gold thing some silver nothing she's not even a chain
you know so people have changed sudha murthy has not i don't know because i really believe
certain amount of i'm not jesus christ i'm not bhagwan buddha i'm not mahatma gandhi we do
require money in life yes how much you require depends on you for somebody one crore is enough
somebody 10 crores is it not enough so my uh expectation in life is very low so i have met
my expectation so the rest of the money i always feel you should go in the service of the people
of the society of my country amazing can we please give a big round of applause to the amazing that's
a standing ovation there for sudha murthy which is amazing and this festival is all about
extraordinary men and women acknowledging other extraordinary men and women so i could think of
nobody better to honor sudha murthy than our next extraordinary guest we are celebrating change
makers today and may i please welcome on stage the incomparable indomitable unconquerable kareena
kapoor come on show us some love guys
yes
so i want to request kareena to please honor sudha murthy with our change maker award
and i want to ask you because you're a big movie buff anything you want to see kareena well i've
seen almost all her movies but i like jabvi met it's very nice chandani chandani bar also is very
good and then you're almost all thank you ma'am thank you big round of applause for sudha murthy
